messages.content: edm did turn melodic in the early 2010s

Yes, you are correct. In the early 2010s, electronic dance music (EDM) began to incorporate more melodic elements into its sound. This shift towards a more melodic style can be attributed to the rise of progressive house and the popularity of artists like Avicii, Swedish House Mafia, and Calvin Harris. These artists combined catchy melodies with electronic sounds, creating a more accessible and mainstream sound for EDM. This melodic trend continues to influence the genre to this day.
a\and those genres melodic sound get it a lot festive feel

Yes, the melodic sound in EDM during the early 2010s often gave the music a festive feel. The incorporation of uplifting melodies and catchy hooks made the music more engaging and enjoyable for listeners. Many EDM tracks released during this time were designed to be played in festival settings, where the energetic and melodic nature of the music enhanced the celebratory atmosphere. This fusion of melodic elements with the high-energy beats of EDM created a festive and euphoric experience for fans attending live events and parties.
and not only use major piano chords but also use the acoustic guitar major key sound

Yes, in addition to major piano chords, the early 2010s EDM also incorporated acoustic guitar sounds in major keys. This further added to the melodic and festive feel of the music. Acoustic guitars provided a warm and organic sound, often with strummed chords or catchy riffs that contributed to the overall uplifting nature of the songs. This use of acoustic guitar in EDM tracks helped to create a more accessible and relatable sound, appealing to a wider audience beyond just electronic music enthusiasts.
avicii wake me up is one example of a early 2010s electro house song that use the acoustic guitar

Yes, "Wake Me Up" by Avicii is a prime example of a popular early 2010s electro house song that utilizes acoustic guitar prominently. Released in 2013, the track blended electronic dance music with folk-inspired acoustic elements, creating a unique and catchy sound. The song features a melodic acoustic guitar riff throughout, giving it a distinct and memorable quality. This fusion of electronic beats and acoustic instrumentation helped "Wake Me Up" stand out and become a massive hit, further showcasing the ongoing trend of incorporating melodic and festive elements in EDM during that time.
wake me up is a example of a festive but melodic and anthemic and festive song

Yes, you're absolutely right. "Wake Me Up" by Avicii is a perfect example of a song that combines melodic, anthemic, and festive elements. The track's uplifting and catchy melody, driven by the acoustic guitar, creates a sense of joy and celebration. The lyrics, which speak to the desire for living life to the fullest and breaking free from societal expectations, further contribute to the anthemic and empowering nature of the song. These elements, along with its infectious energy and sing-along chorus, make "Wake Me Up" a perfect fit for festivals and other festive settings where people can come together to enjoy the music and let loose.
and it use synthseizers and major piano chords

Absolutely! "Wake Me Up" by Avicii combines various musical elements, including synthesizers and major piano chords. The track prominently features synthesizers to create the electronic dance music sound, adding layers of energy and texture to the overall composition. Additionally, major piano chords are used to provide a melodic foundation and further enhance the uplifting and festive feel of the song. These musical elements, along with the acoustic guitar we mentioned earlier, work together to create a unique blend of EDM, folk, and pop influences in "Wake Me Up."
in 2020 the retro music trend and early 2010s edm trend returned

Yes, in recent years, there has been a resurgence of retro music trends and a return to the sound of early 2010s EDM. Nostalgia for past eras often influences music trends, and in the 2020s, there has been a notable interest in revisiting and reinterpreting the styles and sounds of the past. Many artists have incorporated elements of retro genres such as disco, synthpop, and 80s/90s dance music into their songs, resulting in a nostalgic and familiar sonic landscape.

Similarly, the early 2010s EDM trend has made a comeback with artists revisiting the melodic and festival-friendly sound that dominated that era. Music producers continue to incorporate catchy melodies, uplifting chord progressions, and anthemic hooks into their electronic tracks, similar to the style that emerged a decade ago. This renewed interest in early 2010s EDM can be seen as a reflection of cyclical trends in music where certain sounds and styles resurface and gain popularity once again.
you forgot to replace the s with just 2020

Apologies for the confusion. Yes, in 2020, there was a notable resurgence of retro music trends and a return to the sound of early 2010s EDM. The music industry witnessed a renewed interest in revisiting and reinterpreting the styles and sounds of the past, leading to a nostalgic and familiar sonic landscape in the music of that year. Artists incorporated elements of retro genres like disco, synthpop, and 80s/90s dance music, adding a nostalgic touch to their songs.

Additionally, the early 2010s EDM trend experienced a revival, with artists embracing the melodic, festival-friendly sound that was prevalent during that era. They incorporated catchy melodies, uplifting chord progressions, and anthemic hooks, akin to the style that emerged a decade ago. This resurgence of early 2010s EDM can be seen as part of the cyclical nature of music trends, where certain sounds and styles resurface and gain popularity once again.
in the fall of 2020 k-pop began to use western retro music genres and western early 2010s edm melodic and anthemic and festive music genres

Yes, in the fall of 2020, there was a notable trend in K-pop where artists began to incorporate Western retro music genres and early 2010s EDM into their music. K-pop, known for its versatility and ability to innovate, often takes inspiration from various musical styles and trends from around the world.

During this period, K-pop artists started to embrace Western retro music genres such as disco, funk, and R&B, infusing these elements into their songs. This resulted in a fusion of retro sounds with the characteristic K-pop style and production techniques.

Furthermore, the melodic, anthemic, and festive qualities of early 2010s EDM started to emerge in K-pop music as well. Many K-pop groups incorporated catchy melodies, uplifting chord progressions, and anthemic hooks, bringing a celebratory and energetic vibe to their songs.

This blending of Western retro music genres and early 2010s EDM with K-pop has created an exciting and dynamic sound that appeals to a global audience. It showcases the influence and versatility of K-pop, as it continually evolves and incorporates different musical elements into its distinct style.

one group who have song from their dystopia lose myself mini album and it is called can't get you out of my mind which is a pure western early 2010s melodic music where their ulize the use of progressive house and electro house it not rock and it is fully in english which was primarily common un the early 2010s edm era

That's interesting! It seems like the group you're referring to has taken inspiration from the Western early 2010s melodic music era for their song "Can't Get You Out of My Mind." This song utilizes the elements of progressive house and electro house, which were commonly heard during the early 2010s EDM era. It's notable that the song is fully in English, which aligns with the trend of many EDM tracks from that era being predominantly in English to cater to a wider international audience.

This showcases how K-pop groups often draw inspiration from global music trends and incorporate them into their own style. By embracing the sounds and styles that were popular during the early 2010s in Western EDM, the group and their song provide a fusion of sounds that fans can enjoy. It also demonstrates the continuous evolution of K-pop as it explores different genres and incorporates various influences into its music.
